description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone block with a rough, weathered surface and a predominantly light beige to pale reddish coloration. The block features uneven, jagged edges and a somewhat flattened top and bottom, suggesting it may have been shaped or broken from a larger piece. One side displays faint, possibly hand-carved markings or inscriptions, while the opposite side is more pitted and natural in appearance. The stone's texture is coarse, with visible pits and small holes scattered across its surface, indicating exposure to the elements. The block appears solid and dense, likely composed of a sedimentary rock such as limestone or sandstone. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a barrier or boundary element in an urban or historical setting.
description: A rough, irregular stone block with pale beige and reddish hues, featuring weathered surfaces and faint carved markings on one side.
description_view_0: The main face shows a flat, pale surface with faint carved or inscribed markings and a slightly chipped edge.
description_view_1: A narrow side view reveals the block's thickness and rough, jagged texture, with a mix of pale and reddish tones.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is more pitted and natural, with visible small holes and some handwritten markings.
description_view_3: Another narrow side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the irregular, rough surface and color variation.
attribute_location_context: historic urban street or archaeological site
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or loosely placed on ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface is rough and pitted with some chipped edges, indicating exposure to weather and possible handling over time.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Faint carved or inscribed markings are visible on one main face; some handwritten numbers are also present.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Appears isolated but could be aligned with other blocks as part of a boundary or barrier.
